title: potentilla × arctoalaskensis
intro: Potentilla × arctoalaskensis, also known as Arctic Cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between Potentilla fruticosa and P. uniflora. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its high tannin and salicylic acid content, which exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Potentilla × arctoalaskensis have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
